title: How to count unique values that match a condition
task: Count distinct entries in one column, but only for rows matching a criterion in another.

excel.note: Excel 365/2021. COUNTA can replace ROWS if the source has no blanks.

explanation: Working inside-out: FILTER keeps the names on "East" rows (alice, alice, carol, bob), UNIQUE collapses the duplicates (alice, carol, bob), and ROWS counts them — 3 distinct people. The classic pre-dynamic-array alternative =SUMPRODUCT((B2:B7="East")/COUNTIFS(A2:A7,A2:A7,B2:B7,B2:B7)) still works in older versions but is much harder to reason about.
